expected_trades_per_day_basis: Trigger frequency depends on paired entry, but with taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60 filter on pricechange/long entries (typical trig ~2000-3000 over 30d window), expect ~70-130 raw triggers/day, dedupe + symbol cooldown brings to ~4-5/day actually executable.

best_in: Trending up days where initial 0.5% pop sustains into 2-5% continuation (BTC daily range >2%)
High taker-buy aggression regimes (funding flipping positive, perp basis widening)
worst_in: Choppy ranges where 0.5% TP fills then trail-stop on remaining 50% gets whipsawed
Low-vol weekend periods where neither leg gets meaningful follow-through
regime_sensitivity.explanation: The hybrid only outperforms a pure fixed-TP when the trail leg captures meaningful continuation beyond 0.5%; in chop, the trail leg systematically underperforms an all-fixed exit because the 1.5% step is wider than typical post-entry pullback. Expect this archetype to shine in BTC-led directional days and bleed in compressed vol.

live_readiness_caveats: Kernel composition not natively supported — synthesizer flagged downgrade to multi_tp approximation, which historically scored mean=-0.276 and ZERO positive instances in R3, so the 'paper' version may be substantially worse than the design intent.

Partial-fill accounting: 50% TP at 0.5% requires precise position sizing on both legs; on micro-cap symbols with min-notional limits, the trail leg may end up with dust positions that incur disproportionate fees.

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: trail leg < +0.3% mean over rolling 20 trades
monitoring_metrics.rationale: If the trail half doesn't materially beat the fixed half's 0.5%, the hybrid is just a worse pure-fixed (added complexity, same edge) — kill it.
Shadow-trade a pure fixed-TP-only twin in parallel; if hybrid doesn't beat it by a clear margin, the trail leg is pure cost.

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: <25% of trail legs convert to >0.5% realized
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Confirms whether 1.5% step is correctly sized for actual post-pump continuation distribution on pricechange channel.
Two exit fills doubles maker/taker fees vs single-exit families; on $1000 capital with 5-10% sizing, fees can erase the marginal trail edge.
Diverging from the 4.2 estimate signals either filter mismatch (taker_buy_ratio_5m calculation drift) or upstream entry behavior change.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling mean per-trade return < pure-fixed-twin's mean for 2 consecutive 7-day windows
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Hybrid only justifies its complexity if it beats the simpler twin; sustained underperformance means the trail leg is structurally a tax, not an edge.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Max drawdown on the trail-leg sub-PnL exceeds -3% within first 30 trades
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: R2 E126 lesson — asymmetric exits with long-tail downside can compound to large account drawdowns even when win-rate looks healthy; trail leg has the heavier tail and must be policed.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: kernel composition was downgraded to multi_tp approximation AND first 25 trades show median < 0
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Synthesizer's fallback path is the same family that scored zero positives in R3; if the approximation version doesn't beat coin-flip in 25 trades, the design intent is unrecoverable in current kernel set.

expected_max_dd_basis: Symmetric design (50% at +0.5%, 50% trailing with 1.5% step) bounds per-trade left tail tighter than E126's TP=0.51/SL=6.00 trap — but the trail leg adds variance; on 5-10% sizing with ~120 trades over 30d paper window and assumed 60% win rate at +0.4% mean / 40% loss at -1.0% mean, monte-carlo style worst-case rolling DD lands ~4-5%.
compare_to_existing: Most resembles the fixed-exit family (mean +0.175, median +0.217, 91% positive rate in R3) blended with a small dose of multi_tp behavior (mean -0.276, 0% positive) — net expectation is fixed-family-dominant but with a meaningful drag from the trail leg unless market regime is strongly trending.
